description: Myc epitope tag — canonical 10 aa peptide (EQKLISEEDL) from human c-Myc (P01106), recognized by the 9E10 monoclonal antibody for immunodetection of recombinant fusion proteins.

references.authors: Evan GI, Lewis GK, Ramsay G, Bishop JM
references.title: Isolation of monoclonal antibodies specific for human c-myc proto-oncogene product

references.authors: Hilpert K, Hansen G, Wessner H, Küttner G, Welfle K, Seifert M, Höhne W
references.title: Anti-c-myc antibody 9E10: epitope key positions and variability characterized using peptide spot synthesis on cellulose

references.authors: Schüchner S, Behm C, Mudrak I, Ogris E
references.title: The Myc tag monoclonal antibody 9E10 displays highly variable epitope recognition dependent on neighboring sequence context

references.authors: Russo G, Unkauf T, Meier D, Wenzel EV, Langreder N, Schneider KT, Wiesner R, Bischoff R, Stadler V, Dübel S
references.title: In vitro evolution of myc-tag antibodies: in-depth specificity and affinity analysis of Myc1-9E10 and Hyper-Myc

label: Myc
note: Canonical 10 aa Myc epitope tag (EQKLISEEDL) from human c-Myc (UniProt P01106, residues 425-434). Recognized by the 9E10 monoclonal antibody; widely used for immunodetection and immunoprecipitation of recombinant fusion proteins. Atomic part: no functional sub-elements.

provenance.sequence_source: UniProt:P01106 (MYC_HUMAN, Myc proto-oncogene protein, Homo sapiens, Swiss-Prot reviewed, 454 aa); the 10 aa part sequence EQKLISEEDL is an exact sub-sequence at positions 425-434 (1-based), independently re-fetched from https://rest.uniprot.org/uniprotkb/P01106.fasta and confirmed 100% identical

functional_claims.label: The EQKLISEEDL peptide is specifically recognized by the 9E10 monoclonal antibody (anti-c-Myc), enabling immunodetection and immunoprecipitation of Myc-tagged recombinant fusion proteins.

source.quote: monoclonal antibodies specific for human c-myc proto-oncogene product

functional_claims.label: The minimal binding determinant of 9E10 within EQKLISEEDL is KLISEEDL (8 aa); the core residues required for recognition are LISE (positions 4-7 of the full tag). Position substitution analysis shows EQK and the terminal L are partially dispensable.

source.quote: 9E10 core epitope LISE and minimum binding peptide KLISEEDL; substitutional analysis of EQKLISEEDL by peptide spot synthesis

functional_claims.label: 9E10 recognition of EQKLISEEDL is highly context-dependent: neighboring residues in the fusion protein can abolish or reduce antibody binding. Alternative antibodies (4A6, 9B11) show less context sensitivity and are recommended when robust detection across diverse fusion architectures is required.

value.recommendation: test detection empirically; consider alternative anti-Myc antibodies for sensitive applications

source.quote: The Myc tag monoclonal antibody 9E10 displays highly variable epitope recognition dependent on neighboring sequence context

functional_claims.label: Hyper-Myc is an evolved anti-Myc antibody with 18 nM affinity and improved thermal stability (+37%) compared to 9E10, while retaining identical EQKLISEEDL epitope specificity. Existing Myc-tagged constructs can be detected with Hyper-Myc without sequence modification.

source.quote: evolved antibody with 18 nM affinity (+improved vs 9E10) and +37% thermal stability, retaining identical EQKLISEEDL epitope specificity

functional_claims.label: As a small synthetic peptide tag derived from a human protein, EQKLISEEDL functions as an epitope tag across all common expression hosts (bacterial, yeast, insect, mammalian) regardless of the fusion partner orientation (N- or C-terminal).

source.quote: Isolation of monoclonal antibodies specific for human c-myc proto-oncogene product
